eras.name: Kievan Rus' Period
eras.description: Fashion was heavily influenced by Byzantine styles and Norse utility. Clothing emphasized layering for warmth, featuring long tunics and heavy wool cloaks fastened with fibulae.
eras.aiImagePrompt: Historical illustration of a 10th-century Kievan Rus noble wearing a long white linen rubakha with red embroidery, a heavy wool kaftan lined with fur, and leather boots, set in a snowy medieval village.

eras.name: Moscovite Tsardom
eras.description: A period defined by 'Terem' culture and extreme modesty. Garments were voluminous and heavy, using stiff brocades and pearls to signify social status and religious piety.
eras.aiImagePrompt: A 16th-century Russian Boyar in a tall fur hat (Gorlatnaya) and a heavy gold-brocade Feriaz coat with extremely long sleeves, standing in a traditional wooden palace.

eras.name: Petrine Westernization
eras.description: Peter the Great forcibly introduced European fashion, banning traditional Russian dress for the nobility. French and German styles dominated the court, including corsets and wigs.
eras.aiImagePrompt: An 18th-century Russian aristocrat at the court of St. Petersburg wearing a Rococo French-style silk gown with elaborate lace ruffles and a powdered wig.

eras.name: Imperial Golden Age
eras.description: A fusion of European high fashion and Russian motifs. The court uniform was strictly regulated, while the upper class embraced Parisian haute couture and Fabergé craftsmanship.
eras.aiImagePrompt: A late 19th-century Russian noblewoman wearing a velvet evening gown with a matching fur-trimmed mantle and an emerald-encrusted tiara, inspired by Romanov opulence.

eras.name: Early Soviet Constructivism
eras.description: Avant-garde artists like Varvara Stepanova designed functional, geometric clothing for the 'new socialist person'. The focus shifted to mass production and utilitarianism.
eras.aiImagePrompt: A 1920s Soviet worker wearing a constructivist outfit with bold geometric patterns in red, black, and white, designed for industrial labor and mobility.

eras.name: Late Soviet Stagnation
eras.description: Characterized by a shortage of consumer goods and the rise of 'fartsovshchiki' (black market traders). Western denim and synthetic fabrics became ultimate status symbols.
eras.aiImagePrompt: A young person in 1970s Moscow wearing acid-washed denim and a thick wool sweater, standing in front of brutalist architecture.

eras.name: Post-Soviet Renaissance
eras.description: A blend of 90s gopnik subculture, Cyrillic typography, and high-fashion irony. This era saw the global rise of Russian designers reclaiming their heritage through grit and luxury.
eras.aiImagePrompt: Modern Russian streetwear featuring an oversized hoodie with Cyrillic text, high-end sneakers, and a luxury parka, set against a backdrop of neon Moscow city lights.

textiles.name: Vologda Lace
textiles.description: Intricate bobbin lace known for its floral and snowflake patterns, traditionally made in white or cream linen.

textiles.name: Pavlovo Posad Wool
textiles.description: Fine wool shawls featuring vibrant, multi-colored floral prints, often with a distinctive fringe.

textiles.name: Ivanovo Chintz
textiles.description: Historic cotton fabric known for bright, populist patterns used for everyday peasant clothing.

climate.fashionImplication: Extreme seasonality necessitates heavy investment in high-quality outerwear, fur insulation, and layered woolens to survive temperatures reaching -30°C.
